subtitle: A rare dark-sand beach on the Arabian Sea 21 km from Surat, famous as one of India's 'most haunted' places — a reputation its weekend crowds, food stalls, and seaside Ganesh temple cheerfully ignore.
spike_statement: A rare black-sand beach near Surat whose iron-dark sand and a cremation legend made it one of India's 'most haunted' shores, and also a packed weekend picnic spot.

levers.photo_tone: dark gray-black sand against the flat brown Arabian Sea under hazy Gujarat light, food stalls and weekend crowds by day, the seaside Ganesh shrine, nothing actually spooky in daylight
